pi_items.source: Task setup scripts manually reset the preference to ensure standard security level.
Task description instructs changing to Safer or Safest.
Extracted from omni.ja within Tor Browser installation (/tmp/tor-browser/Browser/browser/omni.ja and SecurityLevel.sys.mjs).

privileged_info_summary: The task correctly expects the Tor Browser security level to start at 'standard' and be changed to 'safer' or 'safest' using the 'browser.security_level.security_slider' preference. However, there is a critical bug in the export_result.sh and setup_task.sh scripts: they incorrectly map the slider values (assuming 1=standard, 2=safer, 4=safest), whereas the actual Tor Browser implementation maps them as 1=safest, 2=safer, 4=standard. Because of this inverted logic, if an agent correctly changes the security level to 'Safest' (value 1), the export script will misinterpret it as 'Standard' (no change) and cause the verifier to incorrectly fail the task.
